message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1). Venv: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
/tmp/pass1_reconfirm.json is a JSON array of 10 pass-1 findings that were strengthened after a first confirmation pass found them 'appropriate but incomplete'. Each item has: issue, title, file, problem, fix_update (what was just added/changed).
Constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign, no metered API, engine imports no adapter, lazy optional backends.

You are independent CONFIRMER #1 of 2 for INDEX 1 (0-based) in /tmp/pass1_reconfirm.json.
Read that item (issue, problem, fix_update). Then verify the CURRENT state on branch harden/pass-1: read the changed source AND the named regression test(s), and run a PoC / the specific tests against the venv where useful. Decide: appropriate = the fix soundly addresses the root cause (no hack/over-claim); complete = it fully resolves the finding AND has a regression test that would actually catch a regression (a test that passes equally with/without the fix does NOT count). If the item documents a deliberately-deferred scope (e.g. #119's Protocol-boundary note), judge completeness against that honestly-scoped fix, not an unbounded ideal. Set either false with specifics if not met. Echo the issue number.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
{
  "issue": "111",
  "title": "Graph-orchestration gate decisions never enter the hash-chained provenance trail \u2014 the documented \u201cappend-only trail\u201d for the durable path is unchained, untamper-evident LangGraph state",
  "file": "/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py",
  "sev": "MEDIUM",
  "problem": "There are two governance mechanisms (ADR-0010): the synchronous run_study path records every gate decision into the hash-chained ProvenanceTrail (provenance.py), and the durable LangGraph interrupt/resume path (gatenode.make_gate_node) records decisions as plain dicts appended to a LangGraph state list 'gate_decisions'. GOVERNANCE \u00a73 states the chained ProvenanceTrail \"is the spine\u2026 No step can be retroactively removed or reordered,\" and GOVERNANCE.md lines 47-50 call the graph's gate_decisions ",
  "fix_update": "make_gate_node already accepts recorder= (bridges to ProvenanceTrail); now GOVERNANCE \u00a73 + graph.py docstring honestly scope that the durable graph path needs the recorder to be hash-chained. Test test_gate_node_bridges_decision_into_provenance_trail."
}

content.text: Issue 111. Let me read the source file and the named test.

content.content:
1	"""Human-in-the-loop gate node — the interrupt/resume protocol (GOVERNANCE.md).
2	
3	:func:`make_gate_node` turns an engine :class:`~assay_engine.orchestration.gates.Gate` plus a
4	proposal builder into a LangGraph node that pauses the run at the gate (``interrupt``),
5	surfaces a proposal to the operator, and on resume validates and records the decision.
6	
7	The **gate-correlation guard** rejects a resumed decision aimed at a *different* gate than the
8	one currently parked — a stale or misrouted decision (e.g. delivered late through the
9	governance channel) must not be applied to whatever gate happens to be waiting. The guard's
10	expected gate is the gate the operator named in the resume value, compared against this node's
11	gate; auto-deriving it from the parked gate would make the guard always match and thus a no-op.
12	
13	Two further hardened behaviors, ported faithfully from the prior platform's gate node:
14	
15	- **Re-prompt on a bad resume (not raise).** ``interrupt()`` consumes and *persists* the
16	  resume value into the checkpoint *before* the node returns, so raising on a malformed or
17	  mis-correlated value would strand it in the checkpoint and replay it forever — the gate
18	  would be permanently bricked. Instead the node validates inside a loop and re-fires
19	  ``interrupt()`` (carrying the error) on a bad value, so the operator can re-submit and
20	  actually recover.
21	- **Terminal-decision idempotency.** An ``approved`` decision is terminal: re-entering an
22	  already-approved gate is a no-op (it does not re-prompt or re-record). ``rejected`` and
23	  ``deferred`` are deliberately NOT terminal, so a reject→revise→re-review loop re-fires the
24	  interrupt as intended.
25	
26	The node returns a partial state update appending one record to ``gate_decisions``; a study's
27	graph state should declare ``gate_decisions`` with a list/add reducer so records accumulate
28	across gates. The decision/recording validator (:func:`apply_gate_decision`) is pure and
29	tested offline; only the ``interrupt`` call requires LangGraph (the optional ``orchestration``
30	extra, imported lazily).
31	"""
32	
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping
36	
37	from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import Gate, GateError
38	
39	GateProposal = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], Mapping[str, Any]]
40	
41	_VALID_DECISIONS = frozenset({"approved", "rejected", "deferred"})
42	
43	
44	def apply_gate_decision(gate: Gate, resumed: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
45	    """Validate a resumed operator decision and return the state update to record it.
46	
47	    Raises :class:`GateError` if the resume value is malformed, targets a different gate
48	    (correlation guard), or carries an unrecognized decision.
49	    """
50	    if not isinstance(resumed, Mapping):
51	        raise GateError(
52	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: resume value must be a mapping, got {type(resumed).__name__}"
53	        )
54	    target = resumed.get("gate_id")
55	    if target != gate.name:
56	        raise GateError(
57	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: resumed decision targets {target!r} — stale or misrouted; "
58	            "rejecting (gate-correlation guard)"
59	        )
60	    decision = str(resumed.get("decision", "")).strip().lower()
61	    if decision not in _VALID_DECISIONS:
62	        raise GateError(
63	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: invalid decision {decision!r} "
64	            f"(expected one of {sorted(_VALID_DECISIONS)})"
65	        )
66	    # A rejection/deferral with no rationale is governance-relevant; record a sentinel rather
67	    # than a blank (incl. whitespace-only) so the audit trail is never silently empty.
68	    rationale = str(resumed.get("rationale") or "").strip() or "No rationale provided."
69	    record = {"gate": gate.name, "decision": decision, "rationale": rationale}
70	    return {"gate_decisions": [record]}
71	
72	
73	# Only an approval is terminal: a re-entered approved gate is a no-op, but a rejected/deferred
74	# gate must re-fire its interrupt so a revise→re-review loop can re-prompt the operator.
75	_TERMINAL_DECISIONS = frozenset({"approved"})
76	
77	
78	def already_decided(state: Mapping[str, Any], gate_name: str) -> bool:
79	    """True if ``gate_name`` already holds a terminal (approved) decision in ``gate_decisions``."""
80	    for record in state.get("gate_decisions", []) or []:
81	        if record.get("gate") == gate_name and record.get("decision") in _TERMINAL_DECISIONS:
82	            return True
83	    return False
84	
85	
86	GateRecorder = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], None]
87	
88	
89	def make_gate_node(
90	    gate: Gate,
91	    propose: GateProposal,
92	    *,
93	    recorder: GateRecorder | None = None,
94	) -> Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], dict[str, Any]]:
95	    """Build a LangGraph node that parks at ``gate`` for an operator decision.
96	
97	    ``propose(state)`` returns the proposal payload surfaced to the operator (what the gate is
98	    asking them to approve). The node returns a ``{"gate_decisions": [record]}`` update, or
99	    ``{}`` (no-op) if the gate is already terminally approved.
100	
101	    ``recorder`` (optional) bridges each validated decision into the hash-chained provenance
102	    trail (#111): the durable LangGraph path otherwise records gate decisions only as
103	    (untamper-evident) graph state, so the documented append-only trail would miss them. Pass a
104	    callback such as ``lambda r: trail.record("gate", f"gate {r['gate']}: {r['decision']}", **r)``
105	    so the graph path's approvals/rejections land in the same chained trail as the runner's.
106	    """
107	
108	    def node(state: Mapping[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
109	        if already_decided(state, gate.name):
110	            return {}  # terminal decision exists — do not re-prompt or re-record
111	        try:
112	            from langgraph.types import interrupt
113	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
114	            raise RuntimeError(
115	                "orchestration requires the 'orchestration' extra (langgraph) — not installed"
116	            ) from exc
117	        proposal = dict(propose(state))
118	        current: dict[str, Any] = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal}
119	        # Validate inside a loop: on a bad/mis-correlated resume, re-fire interrupt (next
120	        # resume index) carrying the error rather than raising — a raise would strand the
121	        # bad value in the checkpoint and replay it forever, bricking the gate.
122	        while True:
123	            resumed = interrupt(current)
124	            try:
125	                update = apply_gate_decision(gate, resumed)
126	            except GateError as exc:
127	                current = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal, "error": str(exc)}
128	                continue
129	            if recorder is not None:
130	                for rec in update.get("gate_decisions", []):
131	                    recorder(rec)  # land the decision in the hash-chained provenance trail
132	            return update
133	
134	    return node
135

content.text: Let me read the actual test file and the provenance API to verify the test is sound.

content.content:
1	"""Orchestration graph wiring + gate-node interrupt/resume protocol (offline, langgraph mocked)."""
2	
3	import sys
4	import types
5	
6	import pytest
7	
8	from assay_engine.orchestration.gatenode import apply_gate_decision, make_gate_node
9	from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import Gate, GateError
10	from assay_engine.orchestration.graph import RECURSION_LIMIT, compile_graph, resume, run
11	from assay_engine.orchestration.phases import Phase
12	
13	
14	def _gate() -> Gate:
15	    return Gate(
16	        "gate_2",
17	        Phase.PREREGISTER,
18	        Phase.CONFIRM,
19	        lambda ctx: __import__(
20	            "assay_engine.orchestration.gates", fromlist=["GateDecision"]
21	        ).GateDecision(approved=True, gate="gate_2", reason="ok"),
22	    )
23	
24	
25	# ---- apply_gate_decision (pure) ----
26	
27	
28	def test_apply_gate_decision_records_valid():
29	    upd = apply_gate_decision(
30	        _gate(), {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "Approved", "rationale": "looks good"}
31	    )
32	    assert upd == {
33	        "gate_decisions": [{"gate": "gate_2", "decision": "approved", "rationale": "looks good"}]
34	    }
35	
36	
37	def test_apply_gate_decision_correlation_guard_rejects_other_gate():
38	    with pytest.raises(GateError, match="correlation guard"):
39	        apply_gate_decision(_gate(), {"gate_id": "gate_5", "decision": "approved"})
40	
41	
42	def test_apply_gate_decision_rejects_non_mapping():
43	    with pytest.raises(GateError):
44	        apply_gate_decision(_gate(), "approved")
45	
46	
47	def test_apply_gate_decision_rejects_unknown_decision():
48	    with pytest.raises(GateError, match="invalid decision"):
49	        apply_gate_decision(_gate(), {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "maybe"})
50	
51	
52\[REDACTED]("d", ["approved", "rejected", "deferred"])
53	def test_apply_gate_decision_accepts_all_verdicts(d):
54	    upd = apply_gate_decision(_gate(), {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": d})
55	    assert upd["gate_decisions"][0]["decision"] == d
56	
57	
58	# ---- fake langgraph injection ----
59	
60	
61\[REDACTED]
62	def fake_langgraph(monkeypatch):
63	    captured: dict = {"payloads": [], "resumes": []}
64	
65	    def fake_interrupt(payload):
66	        captured["payloads"].append(payload)
67	        if not captured["resumes"]:
68	            raise AssertionError("interrupt called more times than resumes provided")
69	        return captured["resumes"].pop(0)
70	
71	    class Command:
72	        def __init__(self, resume=None):
73	            self.resume = resume
74	
75	    class StateGraph:
76	        def __init__(self, schema):
77	            captured["schema"] = schema
78	            self.nodes = []
79	
80	        def add_node(self, name, fn):
81	            self.nodes.append(name)
82	
83	        def compile(self, **kw):
84	            captured["compile_kw"] = kw
85	            return ("compiled", tuple(self.nodes))
86	
87	    lg = types.ModuleType("langgraph")
88	    types_mod = types.ModuleType("langgraph.types")
89	    types_mod.interrupt = fake_interrupt
90	    types_mod.Command = Command
91	    graph_mod = types.ModuleType("langgraph.graph")
92	    graph_mod.StateGraph = StateGraph
93	    monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "langgraph", lg)
94	    monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "langgraph.types", types_mod)
95	    monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "langgraph.graph", graph_mod)
96	    return captured
97	
98	
99	# ---- make_gate_node ----
100	
101	
102	def test_gate_node_interrupts_with_proposal_and_records(fake_langgraph):
103	    fake_langgraph["resumes"] = [{"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "approved", "rationale": "ok"}]
104	    node = make_gate_node(_gate(), lambda state: {"hypotheses": state.get("n", 0)})
105	    upd = node({"n": 3})
106	    assert fake_langgraph["payloads"][0] == {"gate_id": "gate_2", "proposal": {"hypotheses": 3}}
107	    assert upd["gate_decisions"][0]["decision"] == "approved"
108	
109	
110	def test_gate_node_bridges_decision_into_provenance_trail(fake_langgraph):
111	    # #111: a recorder lands the durable-path gate decision in the hash-chained provenance trail
112	    from assay_engine.provenance import ProvenanceTrail, verify_records
113	
114	    trail = ProvenanceTrail()
115	    fake_langgraph["resumes"] = [{"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "approved", "rationale": "ok"}]
116	    node = make_gate_node(
117	        _gate(),
118	        lambda state: {},
119	        recorder=lambda r: trail.record("gate", f"gate {r['gate']}: {r['decision']}", **r),
120	    )
121	    node({})
122	    verify_records(trail.entries)  # the bridged decision is in an intact hash chain
123	    gates = [e for e in trail.entries if e.kind == "gate"]
124	    assert gates and gates[0].payload["decision"] == "approved"
125	
126	
127	def test_gate_node_reprompts_on_bad_resume_then_recovers(fake_langgraph):
128	    # issue #G1: a mis-correlated then malformed resume must re-fire interrupt, not raise/brick
129	    fake_langgraph["resumes"] = [
130	        {"gate_id": "gate_9", "decision": "approved"},  # wrong gate -> re-prompt
131	        {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "huh"},  # invalid decision -> re-prompt
132	        {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "approved", "rationale": "ok"},  # good
133	    ]
134	    node = make_gate_node(_gate(), lambda state: {})
135	    upd = node({})
136	    assert upd["gate_decisions"][0]["decision"] == "approved"
137	    assert len(fake_langgraph["payloads"]) == 3
138	    assert "error" in fake_langgraph["payloads"][1]  # re-prompt carried the correlation error
139	    assert "error" in fake_langgraph["payloads"][2]  # and the invalid-decision error
140	
141	
142	def test_gate_node_noop_when_already_approved(fake_langgraph):
143	    # issue #G2: re-entering an approved gate is a no-op (no interrupt, no duplicate record)
144	    fake_langgraph["resumes"] = []  # interrupt must NOT be called
145	    node = make_gate_node(_gate(), lambda state: {})
146	    upd = node({"gate_decisions": [{"gate": "gate_2", "decision": "approved", "rationale": "x"}]})
147	    assert upd == {}
148	    assert fake_langgraph["payloads"] == []
149	
150	
151	def test_gate_node_reprompts_when_previously_rejected(fake_langgraph):
152	    # rejected is NOT terminal — the gate must re-prompt so a revise->re-review loop works
153	    fake_langgraph["resumes"] = [
154	        {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "approved", "rationale": "now ok"}
155	    ]
156	    node = make_gate_node(_gate(), lambda state: {})
157	    upd = node({"gate_decisions": [{"gate": "gate_2", "decision": "rejected", "rationale": "no"}]})
158	    assert upd["gate_decisions"][0]["decision"] == "approved"
159	    assert len(fake_langgraph["payloads"]) == 1
160

90	        return hashlib.sha256(msg).hexdigest()
91	    return hmac.new(secret, msg, "sha256").hexdigest()
92	
93	
94	def _utc_now() -> _dt.datetime:
95	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
96	
97	
98	class ProvenanceTrail:
99	    """An append-only, chained audit trail for one study run.
100	
101	    ``secret`` (>= 16 bytes) keys the chain with HMAC so a serialized trail cannot be forged
102	    without it (see module docstring). ``clock`` injects the timestamp source (deterministic
103	    tests); it must return a timezone-aware ``datetime``.
104	    """
105	
106	    def __init__(self, *, secret: bytes | None = None, clock: Clock | None = None) -> None:
107	        if secret is not None and (not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16):
108	            raise ValueError("provenance secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
109	        self._entries: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
110	        self._secret: bytes | None = bytes(secret) if secret is not None else None
111	        self._clock: Clock = clock or _utc_now
112	        # serialize the read-compute-append so concurrent record() calls cannot interleave and
113	        # corrupt the seq/prev_hash chain (#114)
114	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
115	
116	    def record(self, kind: str, summary: str, **payload: Any) -> ProvenanceEntry:
117	        """Append one entry. Returns it. The only way to add to the trail. Thread-safe."""
118	        if not kind:
119	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance entry kind must be non-empty")
120	        with self._lock:
121	            return self._record_locked(kind, summary, payload)
122	
123	    def _record_locked(
124	        self, kind: str, summary: str, payload: Mapping[str, Any]
125	    ) -> ProvenanceEntry:
126	        seq = len(self._entries)
127	        prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
128	        try:
129	            instant = self._clock()
130	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the clock is an injected dependency
131	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance clock raised: {exc}") from None
132	        if not isinstance(instant, _dt.datetime):
133	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a datetime")
134	        try:
135	            # utcoffset()/astimezone()/isoformat() can raise on a hostile datetime subclass or an
136	            # out-of-range value — keep the typed-error contract (#96).
137	            if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
138	                raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a timezone-aware datetime")
139	            timestamp = instant.astimezone(_UTC).isoformat()
140	        except ProvenanceError:
141	            raise
142	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
143	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance timestamp could not be normalized: {exc}") from None
144	        entry_hash = _digest(seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp, prev_hash, self._secret)
145	        try:
146	            entry = ProvenanceEntry(
147	                seq=seq,
148	                kind=kind,
149	                summary=summary,
150	                payload=payload,
151	                timestamp=timestamp,
152	                prev_hash=prev_hash,
153	                entry_hash=entry_hash,
154	            )
155	        except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
156	            raise ProvenanceError(
157	                f"provenance entry for {kind!r} could not be frozen ({exc})"
158	            ) from None
159	        self._entries.append(entry)
160	        return entry
161	
162	    def as_recorder(self) -> Callable[[Any], None]:
163	        """A :data:`gates.ProvenanceRecorder` bound to this trail (records GateDecisions).
164	
165	        Reads each attribute exactly once and defensively, so a booby-trapped decision object
166	        (a property that raises) surfaces a typed ``ProvenanceError`` rather than a raw error.
167	        """
168	
169	        def _record(decision: Any) -> None:
170	            try:
171	                gate = getattr(decision, "gate", None)
172	                approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
173	                reason = getattr(decision, "reason", "")
174	                evidence = dict(getattr(decision, "evidence", {}) or {})
175	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the decision is on the trust boundary
176	                raise ProvenanceError(
177	                    f"could not read gate decision for provenance: {exc}"
178	                ) from None
179	            self.record(
180	                "gate",
181	                f"gate {gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
182	                gate=gate,
183	                approved=approved,
184	                reason=reason,
185	                evidence=evidence,
186	            )
187	
188	        return _record
189	
190	    @property
191	    def entries(self) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
192	        return tuple(self._entries)
193	
194	    @property
195	    def head(self) -> str:
196	        """The current chain head (last entry_hash, or the genesis if empty)."""
197	        return self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
198	
199	    def __len__(self) -> int:
200	        return len(self._entries)
201	
202	    def verify(self) -> None:
203	        """Recompute the chain; raise :class:`ProvenanceError` on any inconsistency."""
204	        verify_records(self._entries, secret=self._secret)
205	
206	    def to_records(self) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
207	        """Serialize to plain dicts (for a persistent store); re-checkable via :func:`verify_records`."""
208	        return tuple(
209	            {
210	                "seq": e.seq,
211	                "kind": e.kind,
212	                "summary": e.summary,
213	                "payload": dict(e.payload),
214	                "timestamp": e.timestamp,
215	                "prev_hash": e.prev_hash,
216	                "entry_hash": e.entry_hash,
217	            }
218	            for e in self._entries
219	        )
220	
221	
222	def verify_records(
223	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
224	    *,
225	    secret: bytes | None = None,
226	) -> None:
227	    """Verify a sequence of entries forms an intact chain (order, linkage, content).
228	
229	    Pass the same ``secret`` the trail was keyed with; an unkeyed trail verifies with
230	    ``secret=None``. A keyed trail will not verify under the wrong/absent secret.
231	    """
232	    prev = _GENESIS
233	    for i, e in enumerate(entries):
234	        if e.seq != i:
235	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} has seq {e.seq} (reordered or removed)")
236	        if e.prev_hash != prev:
237	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} prev_hash breaks the chain")
238	        recomputed = _digest(e.seq, e.kind, e.summary, e.payload, e.timestamp, e.prev_hash, secret)
239	        if not hmac.compare_digest(recomputed, e.entry_hash):
240	            raise ProvenanceError(
241	                f"provenance entry {i} content was altered, or the wrong key was used (hash mismatch)"
242	            )
243	        prev = e.entry_hash
244	
245	
246	def from_records(
247	    records: "list[Mapping[str, Any]] | tuple[Mapping[str, Any], ...]",
248	    *,
249	    secret: bytes | None = None,

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git diff --stat src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_orchestration_graph.py -q 2>&1 | tail -5
input.description: Confirm restore is clean and full test file passes

content.text:
Clean restore (no diff), all 20 tests pass. My verification is complete.

Summary of findings for issue #111:

1. **Root cause addressed soundly.** `make_gate_node` accepts `recorder=` and, after `apply_gate_decision` validates a resume, invokes `recorder(rec)` for each recorded gate decision (gatenode.py lines 129-131). This bridges the durable LangGraph path's decisions into the same `ProvenanceTrail.record(...)` hash-chain used by the synchronous `run_study` path. No hack — it's a clean injected-callback seam consistent with the existing `as_recorder()` design in provenance.py.

2. **Honestly scoped documentation.** Both the graph.py docstring (lines 61-64, "#111 ... not the hash-chained, tamper-evident trail ... pass a recorder") and docs/GOVERNANCE.md §3 (lines 105-110) now explicitly state the durable graph path records to LangGraph state, NOT the chained trail, unless wired with `recorder=`. This matches the fix_update claim. The fix correctly does NOT over-claim: the recorder remains optional (consistent with the constraints — lazy optional backends, engine not forcing the orchestration extra), and the docs say so plainly.

3. **Regression test is genuine.** `test_gate_node_bridges_decision_into_provenance_trail` (tests/test_orchestration_graph.py lines 110-124) wires a real `ProvenanceTrail` recorder, drives the node, then calls `verify_records(trail.entries)` (proving an intact hash chain) and asserts a `gate` entry with the decision exists. I confirmed it is not a tautology: removing the recorder-bridge loop from gatenode.py makes the test FAIL (`assert ([])`), and restoring it makes it pass. It would catch a real regression.

appropriate = true, complete = true.

input.reasoning: Issue #111: gate decisions on the durable LangGraph path were recorded only as plain dicts in graph state ('gate_decisions'), bypassing the hash-chained ProvenanceTrail that GOVERNANCE §3 calls the integrity spine. APPROPRIATE: the fix is a sound seam, not a hack. make_gate_node accepts an optional recorder= callback and, after apply_gate_decision validates the resumed operator decision, calls recorder(rec) for each recorded decision (gatenode.py lines 129-131), bridging durable-path decisions into the same ProvenanceTrail.record(...) hash chain used by the synchronous run_study path. Keeping recorder optional/lazy is correct given the constraints (lazy optional orchestration backend; engine must not force the extra) — and the docs say so plainly rather than over-claiming. The scoping docs are honest and accurate: graph.py docstring (lines 61-64) and docs/GOVERNANCE.md §3 (lines 105-110) both explicitly state the durable graph path records to LangGraph state, NOT the tamper-evident chained trail, unless wired with recorder=. COMPLETE: it fully resolves the documented (honestly-scoped, opt-in) fix and ships a genuine regression test. test_gate_node_bridges_decision_into_provenance_trail (tests/test_orchestration_graph.py lines 110-124) wires a real ProvenanceTrail recorder, drives the node, then verify_records(trail.entries) proves an intact hash chain and asserts a kind=='gate' entry carrying the decision. I confirmed the test is not a tautology: it passes with the fix, and after I temporarily removed the recorder-bridge loop from gatenode.py it FAILED with assert ([]) (no gate entry landed in the trail); I then restored the file (git diff --stat clean) and all 20 tests in the file pass. The test would therefore actually catch a regression.
input.evidence: gatenode.py lines 86-134: recorder param (line 93), bridge loop lines 129-131 calling recorder(rec); docstring lines 100-106 documents #111 bridge. graph.py lines 61-64: '#111 ... not the hash-chained, tamper-evident trail ... pass make_gate_node a recorder'. docs/GOVERNANCE.md §3 lines 105-110: durable LangGraph gate path records to gate_decisions state 'which is not the hash-chained trail; to bring that path under §3 integrity, wire make_gate_node(..., recorder=...)'. Regression test tests/test_orchestration_graph.py:110-124 uses ProvenanceTrail + verify_records. Test runs: PASS with fix (1 passed). Negative control: removed recorder-bridge loop -> test FAILED (assert ([]) at line 124); restored (git diff --stat shows no change) -> full file 20 passed. provenance.py record() (line 116) and verify_records() (line 222) confirm the recorder lands a chained, re-verifiable entry.

content.text: Verification complete for issue #111. I've submitted my structured output: appropriate = true, complete = true. The recorder bridge soundly addresses the root cause, the GOVERNANCE.md §3 and graph.py docstring honestly scope the opt-in nature, and the regression test is genuine (confirmed it fails when the bridge is removed and passes when restored).
